skill.name: key-takeaways
skill.description: Extracts and summarizes key takeaways from documents, meeting notes, articles, and other text content. Use when the user asks for summaries, bullet points, main points, highlights, or a TL;DR of any document or body of text. Produces structured outputs such as numbered lists, executive summaries, and action items. Supports configurable output formats including JSON export for downstream use.
skill.summary: Extracts structured key takeaways, action items, and decisions from any text content.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 56 potential issues across 5 categories, all of which were manually evaluated and confirmed to be false positives. The 3 high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm alerts matched non-cryptographic text in YAML descriptions and audit metadata. The 45 medium-severity shell backtick execution alerts matched markdown code block fence characters in documentation examples, not actual Ruby shell execution. The 5 low-severity system reconnaissance alerts matched audit validation terminology. The 2 hardcoded URL alerts match a GitHub source attribution link. The 1 filesystem alert matches /dev/null usage in a bash documentation example for output suppression. The skill is a legitimate text summarization tool with no malicious intent, no executable payload, and no dangerous patterns.

content.user_title: Extract Key Takeaways from Research Documents
content.value_statement: Manually extracting key points from lengthy research documents is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automatically identifies and structures the most important conclusions, action items, and decisions from any text content.
actual_capabilities: Extract key points and conclusions from documents, meeting notes, and articles
Generate structured summaries with sections for action items and decisions
Configure output depth, audience level, and number of takeaways
Export results in JSON or plain text format for downstream use
Handle batch processing of multiple documents in a directory
limitations: Cannot extract text from scanned PDFs or images that lack machine-readable text
Does not verify the factual accuracy of claims in source documents
Limited to extractive summarization from provided text content
Very large documents may require additional configuration for optimal results

use_cases.title: Review Clinical Trial Publications
use_cases.description: A medical researcher extracts key findings, statistical results, and conclusions from clinical trial reports to quickly compare treatment outcomes.

use_cases.title: Summarize Meeting Notes
use_cases.description: A project manager converts lengthy meeting transcripts into structured summaries with clear action items, decisions, and key discussion points.

use_cases.title: Extract Academic Paper Highlights
use_cases.description: A student distills key concepts and findings from academic papers into concise takeaways for literature review and study preparation.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Takeaway Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract key takeaways from this document
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a document and wants a quick summary of the most important points

prompt_templates.title: Structured Meeting Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize these meeting notes with separate sections for key points, action items, and decisions
prompt_templates.scenario: User has meeting transcripts or notes and needs a structured output for follow-up

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Tailored Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract 5 key takeaways from this article for a non-technical audience
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to communicate technical findings to stakeholders without domain expertise

prompt_templates.title: Batch Export Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all documents in the reports directory and export the takeaways as JSON files
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to analyze multiple documents and produce machine-readable output for integration

output_examples.input: Q3 review meeting notes: Sales up 15%. Product launch delayed to Q4. Alice to update roadmap by Friday. Budget approved for hiring.
output_examples.output: Key points: Sales increased 15% in Q3. Product launch rescheduled to Q4. Action items: Alice to update roadmap by Friday. Decisions: Budget approved for hiring.

output_examples.input: Medical research abstract: We studied 100 patients. The key finding was significant improvement. Results showed 80% success rate with the new treatment protocol.
output_examples.output: Key findings: The new treatment protocol showed 80% success rate in a 100-patient trial. The key finding was significant improvement. Source word count: 24 words.

output_examples.input: Annual department report covering 12% revenue growth, three new hires, product roadmap with two major releases, and Q1 budget planning.
output_examples.output: Summary: Revenue grew 12% year over year. Three new team members hired. Product roadmap includes two major releases. Action items: Finalize Q1 budget by January 15.

best_practices: Review source text for completeness before extracting takeaways to ensure no content is missed
Configure the number of takeaways to match the length and complexity of the input document
Verify extracted points against the original source when using results for critical decisions
anti_patterns: Relying solely on extracted takeaways without reading the original document for important decisions
Requesting too many takeaways from short documents, which produces redundant or trivial points
Using the skill to analyze documents in languages the underlying model does not support

faq.question: What types of documents can this skill process?
faq.answer: The skill works with any text content including meeting notes, articles, reports, academic papers, and research documents. It requires machine-readable text and cannot process scanned images.

faq.question: Can the skill verify if extracted points are accurate?
faq.answer: No. The skill extracts and summarizes content but does not fact-check or verify the accuracy of the source material. Always review results against the original document.

faq.question: How many takeaways can the skill extract at once?
faq.answer: The number of takeaways is configurable. You can set the max_points parameter to control how many key points are extracted from each document.

faq.question: Does the skill support languages other than English?
faq.answer: The skill can process text in any language supported by the underlying AI model. Performance may vary for languages with limited training data.

faq.question: Can I export the results to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports JSON and plain text export formats. You can specify an output path to save the structured takeaways to a file.

faq.question: What happens if the input document is too long?
faq.answer: The skill processes documents within the context window of the underlying model. Very long documents may need to be processed in sections or with adjusted configuration.
